differential_diagnoses: Acute symptomatic seizure related to metabolic or toxic insult
next_steps: Initiate controlled antihypertensive therapy to lower mean arterial pressure gradually
Administer intravenous antiepileptic medication (e.g., levetiracetam) for seizure prophylaxis
Perform continuous electroencephalography (EEG) to monitor for non-convulsive status epilepticus
Perform lumbar puncture to rule out CNS infection and assess opening pressure, provided no contraindications exist
Check serum electrolytes, BUN, creatinine, and hemoglobin levels to evaluate for triggers such as uremia, severe anemia, or electrolyte derangement
Review and adjust potential seizure-lowering medications or substances
Consult neurology for ongoing management of potential chronic vasculopathy or underlying epilepsy
